key_insight: In molecular MPNNs, performance is driven more by how messages are constructed than by how complex the update step is.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical design rule for molecular property models: prioritize operator choices for message-seed initialization and node-edge fusion before adding more elaborate update modules.
method: Factorized 2D molecular MPNNs into three operator families: message-seed initialization, node-edge fusion, and node update.
Benchmarked 84 operator configurations on 10 MoleculeNet datasets under one shared protocol.
Used statistical tests plus a representation probe on Quinethazone to interpret performance and oversmoothing behavior.
evidence_signal: Across ten datasets, the benchmark found significant family-level effects for message-seed initialization in both tasks, a regression-only effect for node-edge fusion, and no statistically supported effect for the update family.
watch_item: Results are benchmark- and dataset-dependent, so the observed operator advantages may not transfer unchanged to other molecular representation settings or larger-scale pretraining regimes.
paper_title: What drives performance in molecular MPNNs? An operator-level factorial benchmark
